entities.name: THERMAL IMAGERY SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems used to capture genotypic differences in canopy temperature for screening

entities.name: CANOPY TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature of the plant canopy used as an indicator for drought tolerance

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to withstand water-deficit conditions

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The process of water movement through a plant and its evaporation from aerial parts

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seed produced per unit area

entities.name: INFRARED CAMERA
entities.description: The device used to capture thermal images of plant canopies

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the chickpea plant

entities.name: RELATIVELY COOL TEMPERATURE RANGES
entities.description: The RC spectrum used to rank genotypes based on canopy temperature

entities.name: RELATIVELY HOT TEMPERATURE RANGES
entities.description: The RH spectrum observed in plant canopy areas

entities.name: STOMATAL CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The measure of the rate of passage of carbon dioxide entering, or water vapor exiting through the stomata of a leaf

entities.name: ROOT SYSTEM
entities.description: The network of roots, specifically prolific and deeper systems in certain genotypes

entities.name: RAINFED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions where water is supplied only by precipitation

entities.name: ICC 4958
entities.description: A specific chickpea genotype known for its prolific and deeper root system
